reasoning: There is currently no vaccine for Human bocavirus infection (HBoV), which is a common respiratory virus primarily affecting children. Recent web searches (up to the knowledge cutoff of Nov 2025) confirm there is no vaccine available or in late-stage clinical trials. The Wikipedia page 'List of infectious diseases' currently leaves the vaccine column empty for this disease. Vaccine development normally spans several years to decades, and it is exceedingly unlikely that a vaccine for HBoV will be successfully developed, approved, and updated on Wikipedia by any of the resolution dates in late 2025 or early 2026. Therefore, the chance is effectively at the minimum permitted probability (0.02).
